title: centaurea boissieri var. pinae
intro: Centaurea boissieri var. pinae, also known as Pinyon Knapweed, is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its chemical compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Centaurea boissieri var. pinae include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, with caution advised due to its potential allergenic and gastrointestinal side effects.
